definitions.definition: Upper three bits of the 8-bit Response Code number; defines the class of response.
definitions.module_or_section: 5.2 Responses / Figure 9: Structure of a Response Code
definitions.evidence: The upper three bits of the 8-bit Response Code number define the class of response.

definitions.definition: Field in a CoAP message indicating that an Empty Message has code 0.00.
definitions.evidence:
Empty Message
      A message with a Code of 0.00; neither a request nor a response.
      An Empty message only contains the 4-byte header.

definitions.definition: Field in the CoAP header used to identify a request or response; set to a Method Code for requests and to a Response Code for responses.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Method Code for requests; Response Code for responses
definitions.evidence: A request is initiated by setting the Code field in the CoAP header of a Confirmable or a Non-confirmable message to a Method Code... A response is identified by the Code field in the CoAP header being set to a Response Code.

definitions.definition: Certificate field used for authority matching when no SubjectAltName is present.
definitions.evidence: If there is no SubjectAltName in the certificate, then the authority of the request URI MUST match the Common Name (CN) found in the certificate

definitions.definition: The combination of an Internet media type, potentially with specific parameters, and a content-coding, identified by a numeric identifier from the CoAP Content-Formats registry.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: No. 12; Length 0-2; Default (none); Critical: no; Unsafe: no; NoCacheKey: no; Repeatable: no
definitions.evidence:
Content-Format
      The combination of an Internet media type, potentially with
      specific parameters given, and a content-coding (which is often
      the identity content-coding), identified by a numeric identifier
      defined by the "CoAP Content-Formats" registry.  When the focus is
      less on the numeric identifier than on the combination of these
      characteristics of a resource representation, this is also called
      "representation format".

definitions.definition: Specifies the representation format by giving the media type and content coding.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: No default value is assumed in the absence of the option.
definitions.evidence: The representation format is specified by the media type and content coding given in the Content-Format Option, if provided.

definitions.type: decimal ASCII integer; may be a space-separated sequence of decimal ASCII integers when quoted
definitions.definition: A Web Linking attribute for use with RFC6690 that provides a hint about the Content-Formats a resource returns.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Each Content-Format code MUST be in the range 0-65535 (16-bit unsigned integer).
definitions.evidence: The Content-Format code "ct" attribute provides a hint about the Content-Formats this resource returns. ... The value is in the CoAP identifier code format as a decimal ASCII integer and MUST be in the range of 0-65535 (16-bit unsigned integer). ... The Content-Format code attribute MAY include a space-separated sequence of Content-Format codes

definitions.definition: Raw data bytes carried in the signature_algorithms extension.
definitions.evidence: Data (4 bytes): 00 02 04 03

definitions.definition: Field of the Uri-Path Option in the example.

definitions.definition: Destination IP address input used when constructing a URI.
definitions.evidence: Destination IP Address = [2001:db8::2:1]

definitions.definition: Destination UDP port input used when constructing a URI.

definitions.definition: Lower five bits of the 8-bit Response Code number; provides additional detail to the overall response class.
definitions.module_or_section: 5.2 Responses / Figure 9: Structure of a Response Code
definitions.evidence: The lower five bits do not have any categorization role; they give additional detail to the overall class (Figure 9).

definitions.definition: Payload of client or server error responses when no Content-Format option is given; contains a brief human-readable diagnostic message explaining the error situation.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: SHOULD be empty if there is no additional information beyond the Response Code
definitions.evidence: If no Content-Format option is given, the payload of responses indicating a client or server error is a brief human-readable diagnostic message... This diagnostic message MUST be encoded using UTF-8 [RFC3629], more specifically using Net-Unicode form [RFC5198].

definitions.definition: DNS header field shown as the first 16 bits of the DNS header; described as an ID that can be chosen by the attacker.

definitions.definition: Length field for the Option Value in an option instance.
definitions.evidence: Each option instance in a message specifies the Option Number of the defined CoAP option, the length of the Option Value, and the Option Value itself.

definitions.definition: Identifier contained in each CoAP message, used to detect duplicates and for optional reliability; ACK/RST messages use the same Message ID as the related message.
definitions.evidence: Each message contains a Message ID used to detect duplicates and for optional reliability. (The Message ID is compact; its 16-bit size enables up to about 250 messages per second...)

definitions.definition: Request semantic code included in CoAP messages carrying requests.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0.01-0.31; initial values: 0.01 GET, 0.02 POST, 0.03 PUT, 0.04 DELETE
definitions.evidence: CoAP request and response semantics are carried in CoAP messages, which include either a Method Code or Response Code, respectively.

definitions.definition: Header field carrying the message identifier.
definitions.evidence: The client sends a Confirmable GET request ... with a Message ID of 0x7d34. ... Header: GET (T=CON, Code=0.01, MID=0x7d34)

definitions.evidence: |QR|   Opcode  |AA|TC|RD|RA|   Z    |   RCODE   |

definitions.definition: Indicates the difference between the Option Number of this option and that of the previous option, or zero for the first option.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0-12 indicate the Option Delta; 13 indicates an 8-bit extended value follows and gives Option Delta minus 13; 14 indicates a 16-bit extended value follows and gives Option Delta minus 269; 15 is reserved for the Payload Marker.
definitions.evidence: Option Delta:  4-bit unsigned integer.  A value between 0 and 12 indicates the Option Delta. Three values are reserved for special constructs...

definitions.type: 0-2 bytes; 8-bit unsigned integer or 16-bit unsigned integer in network byte order when present
definitions.definition: Extended encoding for Option Delta when the initial Option Delta field has value 13 or 14.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Present as 8 bits when Option Delta is 13 and indicates Option Delta minus 13; present as 16 bits when Option Delta is 14 and indicates Option Delta minus 269.
definitions.evidence: Option Delta (extended) 0-2 bytes; 13: An 8-bit unsigned integer follows the initial byte... 14: A 16-bit unsigned integer in network byte order follows the initial byte...

definitions.definition: Indicates the length of the Option Value, in bytes.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0-12 indicate the length of the Option Value; 13 indicates an 8-bit extended value precedes the Option Value and gives Option Length minus 13; 14 indicates a 16-bit extended value precedes the Option Value and gives Option Length minus 269; 15 is reserved for future use.
definitions.evidence: Option Length:  4-bit unsigned integer.  A value between 0 and 12 indicates the length of the Option Value, in bytes.

definitions.type: 0-2 bytes; 8-bit unsigned integer or 16-bit unsigned integer in network byte order when present
definitions.definition: Extended encoding for Option Length when the initial Option Length field has value 13 or 14.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Present as 8 bits when Option Length is 13 and indicates Option Length minus 13; present as 16 bits when Option Length is 14 and indicates Option Length minus 269.
definitions.evidence: Option Length (extended) 0-2 bytes; 13: An 8-bit unsigned integer precedes the Option Value... 14: A 16-bit unsigned integer in network byte order precedes the Option Value...

definitions.definition: Identifies the defined CoAP option for an option instance. It is delta-encoded from the preceding option instance's Option Number.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: For the first instance, preceding Option Number zero is assumed; multiple instances of the same option use a delta of zero.
definitions.evidence: Each option instance in a message specifies the Option Number of the defined CoAP option... the Option Number for each instance is calculated as the sum of its delta and the Option Number of the preceding instance in the message.

definitions.definition: The value carried by an option instance.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0 or more bytes; exactly Option Length bytes.
definitions.evidence: Each option instance in a message specifies the Option Number of the defined CoAP option, the length of the Option Value, and the Option Value itself.

definitions.type: sequence of zero or more CoAP Options in Type-Length-Value (TLV) format
definitions.definition: Options follow the Header and Token. An Option can be followed by the end of the message, by another Option, or by the Payload Marker and the payload.
definitions.evidence: Following the Token value comes a sequence of zero or more CoAP Options in Type-Length-Value (TLV) format

definitions.definition: Optional message payload following the fixed-length binary header and compact binary options.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: If Payload Marker is absent, zero-length payload; marker followed by zero-length payload is a message format error.
definitions.evidence: CoAP uses a short fixed-length binary header (4 bytes) that may be followed by compact binary options and a payload.

definitions.definition: Length of the payload, calculated from the datagram size.
definitions.evidence: The payload data extends from after the marker to the end of the UDP datagram, i.e., the Payload Length is calculated from the datagram size.

definitions.definition: Indicates the end of options and the start of the payload.
definitions.evidence: If present and of non-zero length, it is prefixed by a fixed, one-byte Payload Marker (0xFF), which indicates the end of options and the start of the payload.

definitions.definition: HTTP request line used to specify a CoAP or CoAPS URI when initiating HTTP-CoAP proxying.
definitions.module_or_section: 10. Cross-Protocol Proxying between CoAP and HTTP
definitions.evidence: This is initiated by specifying a "coap" or "coaps" URI in the Request-Line of an HTTP request to an HTTP-CoAP proxy.

definitions.definition: HTTP request field containing a CoAP or secure CoAP URI for HTTP-CoAP proxying.
definitions.evidence: If an HTTP request contains a Request-URI with a "coap" or "coaps" URI, then the receiving HTTP endpoint ... is requested to perform the operation specified by the request method on the indicated CoAP resource

definitions.definition: Field in the CoAP Header that signals whether a message carries a request, a response, or is Empty.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Possible values are maintained in the CoAP Code Registries. An Empty message has the Code field set to 0.00. Reserved classes mentioned: 1, 6, or 7.
definitions.evidence: Separate from the message type, a message may carry a request, a response, or be Empty.  This is signaled by the Request/Response Code field in the CoAP Header... An Empty message has the Code field set to 0.00.

definitions.definition: Response semantic code included in CoAP messages carrying responses.
definitions.evidence: CoAP request and response semantics are carried in CoAP messages, which include either a Method Code or Response Code, respectively.

definitions.definition: Signature algorithm value carried in the signature_algorithms extension.
definitions.evidence: SignatureAlgorithm: ecdsa (3)

definitions.definition: Certificate subject built from a long-term unique identifier for the device or based on the Fully Qualified Domain Name used as the Host part of the CoAP URI.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: long-term unique identifier such as EUI-64, or FQDN
definitions.evidence: The subject in the certificate would be built out of a long-term unique identifier for the device such as the EUI-64 [EUI64].  The subject could also be based on the Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) that was used as the Host part of the CoAP URI.

definitions.definition: Certificate extension containing fields, including fields of URI type, used to match the authority of the request URI.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: URI type fields containing CoAP URI authorities
definitions.evidence: If the certificate contains a SubjectAltName, then the authority of the request URI MUST match at least one of the authorities of any CoAP URI found in a field of URI type in the SubjectAltName set.

definitions.definition: Certificate field that indicates an algorithm of id-ecPublicKey with namedCurves secp256r1.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: algorithm: id-ecPublicKey; namedCurves: secp256r1; public key format: uncompressed
definitions.evidence: the certificate includes a SubjectPublicKeyInfo that indicates an algorithm of id-ecPublicKey with namedCurves secp256r1 [RFC5480]; the public key format is uncompressed

definitions.definition: Value used to match responses to requests independently from the underlying messages.
definitions.evidence: A Token is used to match responses to requests independently from the underlying messages (Section 5.3).

definitions.definition: Indicates the length of the variable-length Token field.
definitions.evidence: Token Length (TKL):  4-bit unsigned integer.  Indicates the length of the variable-length Token field (0-8 bytes).  Lengths 9-15 are reserved

definitions.definition: A Token value follows the fixed-size header in the CoAP message format.
definitions.evidence: This is followed by a variable-length Token value, which can be between 0 and 8 bytes long.

definitions.definition: Indicates if this message is of type Confirmable, Non-confirmable, Acknowledgement, or Reset.
definitions.evidence: Type (T):  2-bit unsigned integer.  Indicates if this message is of type Confirmable (0), Non-confirmable (1), Acknowledgement (2), or Reset (3).

definitions.definition: Field of the Uri-Path Option carrying the option value.
definitions.evidence: The request includes one Uri-Path Option (Delta 0 + 11 = 11, Length 11, Value "temperature")

definitions.definition: Indicates the CoAP version number.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: MUST be set to 1 (01 binary); other values reserved for future versions.
definitions.evidence: Version (Ver):  2-bit unsigned integer.  Indicates the CoAP version number.  Implementations of this specification MUST set this field to 1 (01 binary).
